hooks: The hard part was never writing a trading bot. It's writing the thing that stops one.
I ran 9 live bots, then killed the whole floor with one command.
Every trading-bot pitch shows you green. Mine shows you the live account — losses included.
Default state of every desk in my fleet: OFF.
Nobody brags about their circuit breaker. That's how you know nobody built one.
If a trading product won't show you a losing day, that tells you everything.
'How much will it make me?' is the wrong question. 'What stops it when it shouldn't be trading?' is the right one.
Real markets don't do 'passive.' Anything that runs unattended needs a guard that can shut it down.
Robinhood shipped an official Agentic Trading MCP. Now the question is whether your agent is wrapped in a kill switch. Mine is.
8 agents. One obsession each. One kill switch over all of them.
Capital guard means the agent literally cannot size past the ceiling you set.
The flex isn't a green screenshot. The flex is a live page I never have to take down.
I'd rather show you a red day and keep my credibility than fake a green one and lose it.
Connecting an unsupervised bot to your brokerage account should scare you. That fear is correct.
A backtest is a story told with hindsight. A live tape is the truth.
Local-first. Your machine, your keys, your kill switch.
One desk's logic goes sideways, its breaker trips, it goes dark alone. The other 7 keep running.
I'm not selling returns. I'm selling a safety system + 8 agents that run on your own Mac.
Start is easy. Stop is the engineering.
Buy from people who show you the tape.

reddit_title: I built this and I'm showing the real (losing) tape — {short}
linkedin_opener:
{hook}

Here's what I learned building it.
